description: Introduce a canonical per-theme TOML source of truth and a generator to produce themes/configs, themes/meta, and themes/themes.json consistently on macOS; remove drift and special-cases before adding new themes like vantablack.
title: Epic: Refactor themes to canonical TOML source + generated app configs

acceptance_criteria: Schema doc and migration contract are committed and unambiguous enough for a fresh-context implementer to execute without prior discussion.
close_reason: Schema + migration contract docs completed (SCHEMA.md, MIGRATION_MAPPING.md, CANONICAL_UPSTREAMS.md).

acceptance_criteria: Spec includes required key list, color format rules, identifier fields, and override structure.
close_reason: Completed canonical TOML schema spec with required keys, color rules, identifiers, override contract, and concrete example.
description: Create schema specification (keys, types, required fields, conventions) for themes/sources/<theme>.toml, with one concrete example.
title: Write canonical TOML schema spec

acceptance_criteria: Mapping table clearly states source->artifact ownership and manual-edit boundaries for every theme file family.
close_reason: Completed migration mapping table documenting source-owned vs generated-owned boundaries and runtime targets for all theme file families.
description: Map current runtime artifacts to generated targets and define which files are source-owned vs generated-owned.
title: Document migration mapping + generation targets

acceptance_criteria: Decision table exists for current themes (tokyo-night, gruvbox, everforest, osaka-jade) with repo+file+variant+pinning strategy and clear rules for future themes.
close_reason: Completed: added themes/sources/CANONICAL_UPSTREAMS.md decision table with repo+file+variant+pinned ref+pinning strategy for tokyo-night/gruvbox/everforest/osaka-jade, plus future-theme source selection and normalization rules; linked from themes/sources/SCHEMA.md and themes/README.md.
description: Decide and record one canonical upstream source per theme (repo/file/ref/variant), plus normalization policy into internal palette keys. Clarify when Omarchy is canonical vs when original theme upstream is canonical.
notes:
Context captured on 2026-03-03 (America/Montreal):

Recommended canonical source per current theme:
- tokyo-night: folke/tokyonight.nvim | variant=night | reference file=extras/ghostty/tokyonight_night
- gruvbox: morhetz/gruvbox | variant=dark-hard | reference file=colors/gruvbox.vim
- everforest: sainnhe/everforest | variant=dark-medium | reference files=palette.md and autoload/everforest.vim
- osaka-jade: basecamp/omarchy | variant=dark | reference file=themes/osaka-jade/colors.toml

Policy recommendation:
- Use per-theme upstream canonical source (not Omarchy-for-all)
- Pin each theme source to repo+file+ref(tag/commit)+variant
- Normalize into internal palette keys, then generate all app artifacts
- Use overrides only for app-specific deviations

Key findings:
- Omarchy does not currently expose a per-theme canonical upstream mapping field in themes metadata/docs; treat Omarchy as canonical set itself unless theme-specific upstream is chosen.
- Local drift spotted in current repo:
  - themes/themes.json tokyo-night has several accent values not present in current tokyonight upstream main
  - everforest bg mapping differs from canonical medium ordering (bg1/bg2)

Upstream repos verified (HTTP 200 on 2026-03-03):
- https://github.com/folke/tokyonight.nvim
- https://github.com/morhetz/gruvbox
- https://github.com/sainnhe/everforest
- https://github.com/basecamp/omarchy
title: Define canonical upstream sources + variant pinning

acceptance_criteria: theme-build supports parse+validate on all source themes and returns non-zero with clear diagnostics on invalid input.
close_reason: Completed parser+validator core: strict TOML loader/model plus check-mode validation CLI now parse/validate all canonical sources and exit non-zero with actionable diagnostics on invalid input. Covered by themes/tests/test_theme_build.py and verified with --check on themes/sources.
description: Build theme-build core that loads TOML theme sources into a normalized model and validates format/required keys with actionable errors.
title: Implement theme-build core parser + validator

acceptance_criteria: Loader returns stable normalized objects for all migrated themes and rejects malformed records.

description: Implement loader/model layer for canonical theme sources, including strict parsing and normalization of color/id fields.
